input.user_input: Add a force majeure clause to this supply agreement. Use the ISDA standard definition as a starting point.

actual:
It appears you didn't attach the supply agreement to your message. However, I have drafted a Force Majeure clause below that you can insert into your agreement. 

This draft adapts the standard ISDA Master Agreement "Force Majeure Event" definition (typically found in Section 5(b)(ii) of the 2002 ISDA) to fit a commercial supply context. It retains the core ISDA elements, specifically the inclusion of "acts of state," the "impossibility or impracticability" standard, and the specific "reasonable efforts" carve-out regarding incurring losses.

***

### **[Section X]. Force Majeure**

**(a) Definition of Force Majeure Event.** For the purposes of this Agreement, a "Force Majeure Event" means any event or circumstance (including, without limitation, any force majeure or act of state) occurring after the Effective Date that:
(i) prevents a party (the "Affected Party") from performing any absolute or contingent obligation to make a delivery or payment, from receiving a delivery or payment, or from complying with any other material provision under this Agreement (or makes it impossible or impracticable for the Affected Party to do so);
